title: docs: define youtube canary record persistence threat model
goal: Define a docs-only persistence threat model for YouTube canary authorization records and audit receipts before repository, route, database, or migration work.
rollbackOrStopCondition: Stop without merge if non-doc/runtime paths change, same-head required checks fail, evidence is stale, or any readiness/approval authority is created.
doneCriteria: safe hash correlation boundary is documented
append-only event model is proposed but not implemented
current-state projection is proposed but not implemented
replay prevention and idempotency are documented
access control, retention, and deletion remain awaiting owner decisions
backup/restore, clock policy, read-time integrity, and failure taxonomy are documented
no runtime, DB, route, repository, migration, package, lockfile, workflow, readiness, compliance, approval, or merge authority change

candidates.summary: docs-only threat model before persistence implementation
implement repository and DB schema now
candidates.rejectedReason: storage authority and owner decisions need a threat model first

candidates.summary: treat safe hashes as storage authority
candidates.rejectedReason: safe hashes are correlation identifiers only

bestOfNEvidence.selectionReason: documents persistence risks without adding runtime or storage authority

risks: persistence record mistaken for execution authority
safe hash correlation mistaken for signature or approval
repository implemented before owner access-control decisions
coveredFailurePaths: record created before receipt remains forbidden
raw rows or diagnostics must not be returned

securityBoundaries: No route, repository, database, migration, package, lockfile, workflow, or contract file is changed.
No GitHub approval review is created.
No runtime, production, legal, or YouTube policy readiness is claimed.

packageVerification.verification: No package, lockfile, runtime dependency, DB driver, or Google SDK changes are introduced.

apiCompatibilitySummary.statement: Docs-only threat model; no route, server, repository, persistence, or product runtime API is changed.

reviewScope.scope: P1 YouTube canary authorization record persistence threat model.
reviewScope.riskSummary: Main risk is treating future persistence as execution, approval, or readiness authority.
reviewScope.verificationOracle: Docs-only diff, evidence checks, self-protection, secret scan, and same-head GitHub checks.

residualRisks: Access control, retention, deletion, and audit retrieval owner decisions remain pending.
Repository, route, DB schema, and migration implementation remain future scoped work.
Real YouTube canary execution remains forbidden and unimplemented.
humanConfirmation: AI review is not human approval.
AI review is not GitHub approval review.
This PR does not create owner approval record.
This PR does not create merge authority.

risks.risk: Future persistence could be mistaken for execution authority.
risks.mitigation: Threat model states stored records remain non-executable and cannot create owner approval or readiness.

risks.risk: Access control, retention, and deletion decisions are not finalized.
risks.mitigation: Evidence marks these statuses as awaiting owner decisions and future code must fail closed.

productionLikeRules: AI review is not owner approval.
Stored records do not authorize execution.
Runtime, production, legal, and YouTube policy readiness require future owner-scoped evidence.
